category: Cloud Compute PaaS and App Hosting
description: Virtual machines, container platforms, and platform-as-a-service offerings for running applications in the cloud.

entries.name: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
entries.summary: The largest cloud provider, with hundreds of services spanning compute, storage, databases, ML, and networking.
entries.freeTier: 750 hrs/month t2.micro/t3.micro EC2 (12 months), 5GB S3 standard storage, 1M Lambda requests/month always-free, 25GB DynamoDB always-free.
entries.notes: Free tier mixes 12-month time-limited offers with always-free tiers; check expiration per service.

entries.name: Google Cloud Platform
entries.summary: Google's cloud platform with strong Kubernetes (GKE), BigQuery, and AI/ML offerings, plus a generous always-free tier.
entries.freeTier: 1 non-preemptible e2-micro VM/month, 5GB Cloud Storage, 2M Cloud Functions invocations/month, $300 in trial credits for 90 days.

entries.name: Microsoft Azure
entries.summary: Microsoft's cloud platform, strong on enterprise integration, .NET tooling, and hybrid cloud.
entries.freeTier: 1 B1S Linux/Windows VM (12 months), 1M Azure Functions requests/month always-free, 5GB Blob Storage, $200 credit for 30 days.

entries.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
entries.summary: Notable for the most generous always-free compute tier among major clouds, including ARM-based instances.
entries.freeTier: 4 Arm-based Ampere A1 cores with 24GB RAM always-free, 2 AMD micro VMs, 200GB block storage, 10TB egress/month.
entries.notes: The Ampere A1 always-free allocation is the largest free compute offer of any major cloud as of 2026.

entries.name: IBM Cloud
entries.summary: IBM's cloud platform with Lite-tier services spanning Kubernetes, Cloud Functions, Watson AI, and databases.
entries.freeTier: Lite plans free indefinitely for many services (limited compute/storage quotas, no time limit).

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers / Pages
entries.summary: Edge compute (Workers) and static site/full-stack hosting (Pages) running on Cloudflare's global network.
entries.freeTier: 100,000 Workers requests/day, Pages: 500 builds/month, 100 custom domains, unlimited bandwidth.

entries.name: Render
entries.summary: Modern Heroku-style PaaS for web services, static sites, cron jobs, and managed Postgres/Redis.
entries.freeTier: Free web services (spin down after 15 min idle), free static site hosting with unlimited bandwidth.

entries.name: Fly.io
entries.summary: Deploys Docker containers close to users across a global network of regions, with a focus on low-latency apps.
entries.freeTier: Small allowance of free compute/storage; exact quotas vary, check current pricing page.
entries.notes: Free tier terms have changed multiple times; always confirm on the pricing page before relying on it.

entries.name: Railway
entries.summary: Developer-friendly PaaS for deploying apps, databases, and cron jobs directly from GitHub with minimal config.
entries.freeTier: Trial credit for new accounts; ongoing usage-based Hobby plan with small monthly included usage.

entries.name: Vercel
entries.summary: Frontend cloud built for Next.js and other frameworks, with instant global deploys and edge functions.
entries.freeTier: Hobby plan free for personal projects: 100GB bandwidth/month, serverless + edge functions included.

entries.name: Netlify
entries.summary: JAMstack hosting platform with CI/CD, serverless functions, and form handling built in.
entries.freeTier: 100GB bandwidth/month, 300 build minutes/month, 125k serverless function invocations/month.

entries.name: Heroku
entries.summary: One of the original app PaaS platforms; removed its free tier in 2022, now entry-level paid only.
entries.freeTier: N/A — no perpetual free tier since November 2022.
entries.notes: Included for comparison/migration context; previously the most common 'free dev hosting' reference.

entries.name: Koyeb
entries.summary: Serverless platform for deploying APIs, web apps, and databases across global regions with autoscaling.
entries.freeTier: Free tier includes limited compute (e.g., one small always-on service).

entries.name: PythonAnywhere
entries.summary: Browser-based Python development and hosting environment with a built-in console, editor, and scheduler.
entries.freeTier: One free web app on a subdomain, limited CPU seconds/day, 512MB storage.

entries.name: Glitch
entries.summary: In-browser collaborative coding and instant hosting, popular for prototypes, bots, and small Node/Python apps.
entries.freeTier: Free apps with limited always-on uptime, community remixing.
entries.paidFrom: Pro plan adds boosted apps and custom domains.
